description: skill/SKILL.md has no YAML frontmatter; `name` field is absent, breaking skill registration and display in Claude Code
suggested_fix: Add frontmatter block with `name: codetape` and `description: ...` at top of file

description: skill/SKILL.md has no YAML frontmatter; `description` field is absent, blocking skill summary in plugin registries and @ autocomplete
suggested_fix: Add frontmatter block with `description: Code Historian skill — records semantic traces and keeps docs in sync with code`

description: skills/codetape/SKILL.md (npm-distributed copy) has no YAML frontmatter; `name` field absent, affecting all users who install via npx codetape install
suggested_fix: Add frontmatter block with `name: codetape` matching the skill/SKILL.md fix

description: skills/codetape/SKILL.md (npm-distributed copy) has no YAML frontmatter; `description` field absent
suggested_fix: Add frontmatter block with `description: Code Historian skill — records semantic traces and keeps docs in sync with code`

description: CORS wildcard header on /api/data and /events endpoints allows any browser tab to cross-origin-fetch local trace data from the running dashboard server
suggested_fix: Replace `'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': '*'` with `'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': 'http://localhost'` or remove the header entirely for localhost-only use

description: exec() used to open browser with template string; both variables are controlled (hardcoded platform command + parseInt'd port) so exploitation is not feasible, but exec() spawns a shell unnecessarily
suggested_fix: Replace exec(`${openCmd} ${url}`) with execFile(openCmd, [url]) to eliminate shell invocation

description: External mermaid.js loaded from cdn.jsdelivr.net at major version @10 without Subresource Integrity hash; CDN compromise would inject malicious JavaScript into the local dashboard
suggested_fix: Add integrity='sha384-...' and crossorigin='anonymous' attributes to the mermaid script tag, or bundle mermaid locally

description: Command file declares no `allowed-tools` frontmatter; uses Read (config, component-map), Bash (git diff), and Write (trace file, component-map update)
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools: [Read, Write, Bash]` to frontmatter

description: Command file declares no `allowed-tools` frontmatter; uses Read (config, traces) and Write (sync targets)
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools: [Read, Write]` to frontmatter

description: Command file declares no `allowed-tools` frontmatter; uses Read (component-map) and Write (docs/ARCHITECTURE.md)
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools: [Read, Write]` to frontmatter

description: Command file declares no `allowed-tools` frontmatter; uses Read (component-map, config) and Write (drift.json)
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools: [Read, Write]` to frontmatter

description: Command file declares no `allowed-tools` frontmatter; uses Read (manifest files), Write (.codetape/ structure), and Bash (directory creation via MkDir)
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools: [Read, Write, Bash]` to frontmatter

description: Command file declares no `allowed-tools` frontmatter; uses Read (trace files in .codetape/traces/)
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools: [Read]` to frontmatter

description: Command file declares no `allowed-tools` frontmatter; uses Read (trace file) and Bash (git add + git commit in step 7)
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools: [Read, Bash]` to frontmatter

description: Identical to commands/trace.md — no `allowed-tools` frontmatter declared
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools: [Read, Write, Bash]` to frontmatter

description: Identical to commands/trace-sync.md — no `allowed-tools` frontmatter declared
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools: [Read, Write]` to frontmatter

description: Identical to commands/trace-map.md — no `allowed-tools` frontmatter declared
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools: [Read, Write]` to frontmatter

description: Identical to commands/trace-review.md — no `allowed-tools` frontmatter declared
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools: [Read, Write]` to frontmatter

description: Identical to commands/trace-init.md — no `allowed-tools` frontmatter declared
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools: [Read, Write, Bash]` to frontmatter

description: Identical to commands/trace-log.md — no `allowed-tools` frontmatter declared
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools: [Read]` to frontmatter

description: Identical to commands/trace-commit.md — no `allowed-tools` frontmatter declared
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools: [Read, Bash]` to frontmatter

description: "well-structured" is a vague qualitative term with no measurable definition in this context
suggested_fix: Replace with the concrete format described in step 4: "Generate a conventional commit message following the type(scope): description format"

description: "concise summary derived from trace summary" — "concise" is unmeasured; the trace summary itself is already constrained to 2-3 sentences
suggested_fix: Replace with "one-sentence description derived from the trace summary"

description: "well-structured" is a vague qualitative term (identical file to commands/trace-commit.md)
suggested_fix: Replace with the concrete format described in step 4: "Generate a conventional commit message following the type(scope): description format"

description: "concise summary" — unmeasured qualifier (identical file to commands/trace-commit.md)
suggested_fix: Replace with "one-sentence description derived from the trace summary"

description: "likely component roots" — "likely" is vague; no criterion given for what makes a root more or less likely
suggested_fix: Replace with "inferred component roots (directories containing source files matching detected language patterns)"

description: "likely component roots" — vague (identical file to commands/trace-init.md)
suggested_fix: Replace with "inferred component roots (directories containing source files matching detected language patterns)"

description: "appropriate template" in step 4c — "appropriate" is undefined; the mapping is actually deterministic (README→readme_section.md, etc.)
suggested_fix: Remove the word "appropriate" — the template mapping is already enumerated in the following bullet list

description: "appropriate template" — vague (identical file to commands/trace-sync.md)
suggested_fix: Remove the word "appropriate" — the template mapping is already enumerated in the following bullet list

description: Command takes an optional query argument ($ARGUMENTS) but provides no fallback behaviour for invocation with no arguments; the skill/SKILL.md routing section says to show all traces, but the command file itself is silent
suggested_fix: Add a step 0 or note: "If called with no arguments, show an overview of all traces (date, slug, summary) in reverse chronological order"

description: Identical to commands/trace-log.md — no empty-input fallback described
suggested_fix: Add a step 0 or note: "If called with no arguments, show an overview of all traces in reverse chronological order"

description: plugin.json has no `commands` array; the commands/ directory is present in the repo but may not be auto-discovered by Claude Code's plugin installation path, leaving commands unregistered for plugin installs
suggested_fix: Add a `commands` field to plugin.json listing the command markdown files, or verify that Claude Code's plugin loader discovers commands/ by convention

description: commands/ and skill/commands/ contain identical copies of all seven command files; copy-skill.js only reads from skill/commands/, making commands/ an orphan that can silently drift on future edits
suggested_fix: Remove the top-level commands/ directory and rely solely on skill/commands/, or add a CI check asserting both trees are identical

description: All command files reference @references/* and @templates/* paths that only resolve after the skill is installed; commands used standalone (before install) will silently skip these context docs with no error message
suggested_fix: Add a prerequisite note at the top of each command: "Requires Codetape skill installed at .claude/skills/codetape/"
